options.name: --api-host
options.description: The host address for the Doppler API (default "https://api.doppler.com")

options.name: --dashboard-host
options.description: The host address for the Doppler Dashboard (default "https://dashboard.doppler.com")

options.name: --dns-resolver-address
options.description: Address to use for DNS resolution (default "1.1.1.1:53")

options.name: --dns-resolver-proto
options.description: Protocol to use for DNS resolution (default "udp")

options.name: --enable-dns-resolver
options.description: Bypass the OS's default DNS resolver

options.name: --no-check-version
options.description: Disable checking for Doppler CLI updates

options.name: --no-read-env
options.description: Do not read config from the environment

options.name: --no-verify-tls
options.description: Do not verify the validity of TLS certificates on HTTP requests (not recommended)

options.name: --scope
options.description: The directory to scope your config to (default ".")

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: Run a command with secrets injected into the environment

options.name: --api-host
options.description: The host address for the Doppler API (default "https://api.doppler.com")

options.name: --dashboard-host
options.description: The host address for the Doppler Dashboard (default "https://dashboard.doppler.com")

options.name: --dns-resolver-address
options.description: Address to use for DNS resolution (default "1.1.1.1:53")

options.name: --dns-resolver-proto
options.description: Protocol to use for DNS resolution (default "udp")

options.name: --enable-dns-resolver
options.description: Bypass the OS's default DNS resolver

options.name: --no-check-version
options.description: Disable checking for Doppler CLI updates

options.name: --no-read-env
options.description: Do not read config from the environment

options.name: --no-verify-tls
options.description: Do not verify the validity of TLS certificates on HTTP requests (not recommended)

options.name: --scope
options.description: The directory to scope your config to (default ".")

options.name: --fallback-only
options.description: Read all secrets directly from the fallback file, without contacting Doppler. secrets will not be updated. (implies --fallback-readonly)

options.name: f
options.description: Path to the fallback file. encrypted secrets are written to this file after each successful fetch. secrets will be read from this file if subsequent connections are unsuccessful

options.name: --fallback-readonly
options.description: Disable modifying the fallback file. secrets can still be read from the file

options.name: --no-cache
options.description: Disable using the fallback file to speed up fetches. the fallback file is only used when the API indicates that it's still current

options.name: --no-exit-on-write-failure
options.description: Do not exit if unable to write the fallback file

options.name: --no-fallback
options.description: Disable reading and writing the fallback file

options.name: --passphrase
options.description: Passphrase to use for encrypting the secrets file. the default passphrase is computed using your current configuration

options.name: --forward-signals
options.description: Forward signals to the child process (defaults to false when STDOUT is a TTY)

options.name: --preserve-env
options.description: Ignore any Doppler secrets that are already defined in the environment. this has potential security implications, use at your own risk

options.name: --api-host
options.description: The host address for the Doppler API (default "https://api.doppler.com")

options.name: --dashboard-host
options.description: The host address for the Doppler Dashboard (default "https://dashboard.doppler.com")

options.name: --dns-resolver-address
options.description: Address to use for DNS resolution (default "1.1.1.1:53")

options.name: --dns-resolver-proto
options.description: Protocol to use for DNS resolution (default "udp")

options.name: --enable-dns-resolver
options.description: Bypass the OS's default DNS resolver

options.name: --no-check-version
options.description: Disable checking for Doppler CLI updates

options.name: --no-read-env
options.description: Do not read config from the environment

options.name: --no-verify-tls
options.description: Do not verify the validity of TLS certificates on HTTP requests (not recommended)

options.name: --scope
options.description: The directory to scope your config to (default ".")

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Do not delete anything, print what would have happened

options.name: --max-age
options.description: Delete fallback files that exceed this age (default 336h0m0s)

options.name: --api-host
options.description: The host address for the Doppler API (default "https://api.doppler.com")

options.name: --scope

options.name: --fallback-only
options.description: Read all secrets directly from the fallback file, without contacting Doppler. secrets will not be updated. (implies --fallback-readonly)

options.name: f
options.description: Path to the fallback file. encrypted secrets are written to this file after each successful fetch. secrets will be read from this file if subsequent connections are unsuccessful

options.name: --fallback-passphrase
options.description: Passphrase to use for encrypting the fallback file. by default the passphrase is computed using your current configuration

options.name: --fallback-readonly
options.description: Disable modifying the fallback file. secrets can still be read from the file

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format. one of json, env, yaml, docker, env-no-quotes (default "json")

options.name: --no-cache
options.description: Disable using the fallback file to speed up fetches. the fallback file is only used when the API indicates that it's still current

options.name: --no-exit-on-write-failure
options.description: Do not exit if unable to write the fallback file

options.name: --no-fallback
options.description: Disable reading and writing the fallback file

options.name: --passphrase
options.description: Passphrase to use for encrypting the secrets file. the default passphrase is computed using your current configuration
